new_classes.definition_en: Wine which, when kept at a temperature of 20 °C in closed containers, has an excess pressure of not less than 3 bar (as defined in CN subheading note 1 for subheading 2204 10).

A quality borne by a beverage in a closed container representing the internal gauge pressure above ambient atmospheric pressure, measured in bar at 20 °C, used to discriminate sparkling wine (≥ 3 bar) from other wines in CN subheading 2204 10.

new_classes.label_en: sweetened or flavoured water for direct consumption
new_classes.label_de: gezuckertes oder aromatisiertes Wasser zum unmittelbaren Verbrauch
new_classes.definition_en: Water, including mineral water and aerated water, to which sugar, other sweetening matter, or flavouring has been added, provided it is intended for direct consumption as a beverage, as covered by CN subheading 2202 10 00.

new_classes.label_de: Zubereitung aus Erzeugnissen des Kapitels 22 für Kochzwecke
new_classes.definition_en: A product derived from Chapter 22 goods (other than heading 2209) that has been prepared for culinary purposes and thereby rendered unsuitable for consumption as a beverage; excluded from Chapter 22 and generally classified under heading 2103.

new_properties.label_en: is for direct consumption as a beverage

coverage_explanation:
The axioms cover the following distinguishing criteria from the legal notes:
1. COVERED – Sparkling wine definition (subheading note 1): SparklingWine class with ExcessPressureQuality bearing ≥ 3 bar at 20 °C in closed containers.
2. COVERED – Sweetened/flavoured water for direct consumption (additional note 1 / subheading 2202 10 00): SweetenedOrFlavouredWater class with isForDirectConsumptionAsBeverage flag.
3. COVERED – Exclusion of culinary preparations (chapter note 1(a)): CulinaryPreparation class modelled as complement to beverages for direct consumption.
4. PARTIALLY COVERED – Container volume measurement rule (general note): The property liquidVolumeInLitres is introduced to represent liquid volume (not container capacity), and the existing SmallContainer/LargeContainer TBox classes already capture the 2-litre threshold. However, the semantic distinction between 'liquid volume' and 'container capacity' cannot be fully axiomatised in OWL-DL without punning, so only partial coverage is achieved.
5. NOT COVERED – Additional note 2 on 'actual alcoholic strength by volume' for headings 2204, 2205, and subheading 2206 00 10: The definition of actual vs. potential alcoholic strength and total alcoholic strength involves complex numerical relationships and legal definitions not easily reduced to simple OWL restrictions without detailed sub-concepts (actual vs. potential ABV). This gap reduces coverage.
6. NOT COVERED – Exclusions for sea water (2501), distilled/conductivity water (2853), and acetic acid (truncated note) are cross-references only and carry no product-discriminating OWL content beyond what the chapter note text states.
7. NOT COVERED – Section note on 'pellets' (not specific to Chapter 22 beverages, applies section-wide) — no beverage-discriminating content.
Overall coverage is estimated at 0.72.
